summary: Verified the 'Enable sound' bug fix on the Royal Spark homepage hero video on desktop (1280x900) and mobile (390x844). The underlying React state/handler fix IS working: when handleToggleSound actually executes, video.muted flips to false and STAYS false (no revert), video.volume === 1, and the button label correctly toggles 'Enable sound' <-> 'Mute sound'. However, a NEW (desktop-only) UI regression was discovered: the ENABLE SOUND button is unclickable on desktop because a sibling text-overlay div intercepts pointer events over the entire top strip of the hero. Mobile passes fully.

ui_bugs.issue: ENABLE SOUND button (data-testid='hero-video-sound-toggle') is covered by a transparent sibling overlay div, making it unclickable. document.elementFromPoint at the button's center returns the overlay div, NOT the button. Native Playwright .click() (no force) times out because the element is not the actionable target; only document.querySelector(btn).click() via JS works.

integration_issues.issue: Real desktop users cannot toggle audio on the hero video because the button is blocked by the text-overlay div. The handler itself is correct (verified via JS .click()) but the click never reaches the button via mouse input.

action_items: Fix desktop overlay click-intercept on HomePage.jsx hero. Recommended fix: at line 115 of /app/frontend/src/pages/HomePage.jsx, add 'pointer-events-none' to the outer wrapper div ('absolute left-0 right-0 top-0 hidden px-5 py-5 md:block md:px-8 md:py-8') and add 'pointer-events-auto' to the inner 'max-w-xl' card so the button at top-right remains clickable. Alternative: swap DOM order so the sound-toggle div is rendered AFTER the text overlay, or give the sound-toggle wrapper (line 105) a higher z-index (e.g. z-10).
Re-run the desktop click test after the fix to confirm video.muted toggles to false and stays false via a real (non-force) click.
critical_code_review_comments: HomePage.jsx line 105 vs line 115: two sibling absolute-positioned divs both at top-0 with overlapping bounding boxes. The later one (text overlay) wins for pointer-events on desktop. This is the root cause of the desktop click-intercept regression.

The sound-toggle handler (handleToggleSound) is correctly implemented: it reads from videoElement.muted (DOM), writes back to .muted directly, sets volume=1 on unmute, and uses an uncontrolled 'muted' attribute on the <video> tag so React re-renders no longer revert the muted state. The original 'mute revert' bug is genuinely fixed.

success_rate.frontend: Mobile 100% pass, Desktop 50% (handler logic verified, but button unreachable via real click)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Once the desktop overlay is fixed: (1) On desktop 1280x900, navigate to '/', read video.muted (expect true), click [data-testid='hero-video-sound-toggle'] WITHOUT force=True, then read video.muted immediately and again ~1.5s later — both must be false. (2) volume must equal 1. (3) Button label must change from 'ENABLE SOUND' to 'MUTE SOUND'. (4) Click again -> muted true and label back to 'ENABLE SOUND'. (5) Repeat the same on mobile 390x844 (already passing today). (6) Verify hero wrapper bounding box still has height > 100 on both viewports (no layout regression).
rca_of_the_issue: Reproduction: On desktop 1280x900, load https://spark-mobile-fix.preview.emergentagent.com/, wait for [data-testid='hero-full-video']. Use page.evaluate to call document.elementFromPoint at the center of the ENABLE SOUND button — it returns the sibling overlay div with class 'absolute left-0 right-0 top-0 hidden px-5 py-5 md:block md:px-8 md:py-8', NOT the button. A non-forced Playwright click times out. video.muted remains true. When the button is invoked via JS (btn.click()), the handler runs correctly: muted -> false, volume = 1, label -> 'MUTE SOUND', and the state persists across the 1.5s React re-render window (confirming the original React controlled-prop revert bug IS fixed). Root cause of remaining desktop issue: in /app/frontend/src/pages/HomePage.jsx, two absolute-positioned sibling divs (one wrapping the sound button at line 105, one wrapping the headline text at line 115) overlap at the top of the hero on md+ breakpoints. The text overlay div is rendered later in the DOM, so it sits on top in stacking order and steals pointer events across the full top strip — including the button area on the right. Mitigation: add pointer-events-none on the outer text overlay wrapper and pointer-events-auto on the inner max-w-xl card (cleanest fix), OR raise z-index of the sound-toggle wrapper, OR reorder so the toggle div comes after the text overlay div in the DOM.
